Checks: 7 0

Knit directory: dental-genetics-susie/

This reproducible R Markdown analysis was created with workflowr (version 1.7.1). The Checks tab describes the reproducibility checks that were applied when the results were created. The Past versions tab lists the development history.

R Markdown file: up-to-date

Great! Since the R Markdown file has been committed to the Git repository, you know the exact version of the code that produced these results.

Environment: empty

Great job! The global environment was empty. Objects defined in the global environment can affect the analysis in your R Markdown file in unknown ways. For reproduciblity it’s best to always run the code in an empty environment.

Seed: set.seed(20260523)

The command set.seed(20260523) was run prior to running the code in the R Markdown file. Setting a seed ensures that any results that rely on randomness, e.g. subsampling or permutations, are reproducible.

Session information: recorded

Great job! Recording the operating system, R version, and package versions is critical for reproducibility.

Cache: none

Nice! There were no cached chunks for this analysis, so you can be confident that you successfully produced the results during this run.

File paths: relative

Great job! Using relative paths to the files within your workflowr project makes it easier to run your code on other machines.

Repository version: 67026cf

Great! You are using Git for version control. Tracking code development and connecting the code version to the results is critical for reproducibility.

The results in this page were generated with repository version 67026cf. See the Past versions tab to see a history of the changes made to the R Markdown and HTML files.

Note that you need to be careful to ensure that all relevant files for the analysis have been committed to Git prior to generating the results (you can use wflow_publish or wflow_git_commit). workflowr only checks the R Markdown file, but you know if there are other scripts or data files that it depends on. Overview

This analysis applies SuSiE-RSS fine-mapping to dental GWAS summary statistics and LD matrices.

The current workflow is implemented for DMFS and Nteeth and includes:

DMFS

  1. SuSiE credible set results
  2. Positional mapping to nearest hg19 genes
  3. eQTL colocalization summary

Nteeth

  1. SuSiE credible set results
  2. Positional mapping to nearest hg19 genes
  3. eQTL colocalization summary
library(dplyr)
library(DT)

DMFS

DMFS SuSiE credible set results

dmfs_susie_final_summary <- read.csv("results/dmfs_susie_final_summary.csv")

dmfs_susie_final_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Locus,
    MarkerName,
    CS,
    PIP,
    Purity,
    everything()
  ) %>%
  datatable(
    extensions = "Buttons",
    caption = "DMFS SuSiE Credible Set Results",
    options = list(
      dom = "Blfrtip",
      buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel", "pdf", "print"),
      lengthMenu = list(
        c(10, 25, 50, -1),
        c(10, 25, 50, "All")
      ),
      scrollX = TRUE
    )
  )

DMFS position mapping

dmfs_positional_mapping <- read.csv("results/dmfs_positional_mapping.csv")

dmfs_positional_mapping %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Locus,
    MarkerName,
    PIP,
    external_gene_name,
    ensembl_gene_id,
    everything()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>%
  datatable(
    caption = "DMFS SuSiE Results with Nearest hg19 Genes",
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = list(
      dom = "Blfrtip",
      buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel", "pdf", "print"),
      lengthMenu = list(
        c(10, 25, 50, -1),
        c(10, 25, 50, "All")
      ),
      scrollX = TRUE
    )
  )

DMFS eQTL colocalization

dmfs_eqtl_summary <- readLines("results/dmfs_eqtl_colocalization_summary.txt")

cat(dmfs_eqtl_summary)
No eQTL matches found in the specified tissue: Whole_Blood

Nteeth

Nteeth SuSiE credible set results

nteeth_susie_final_summary <- read.csv("results/nteeth_susie_final_summary.csv")

nteeth_susie_final_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Locus,
    MarkerName,
    CS,
    PIP,
    Purity,
    everything()
  ) %>%
  datatable(
    extensions = "Buttons",
    caption = "Nteeth SuSiE Credible Set Results",
    options = list(
      dom = "Blfrtip",
      buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel", "pdf", "print"),
      lengthMenu = list(
        c(10, 25, 50, -1),
        c(10, 25, 50, "All")
      ),
      scrollX = TRUE
    )
  )

Nteeth position mapping

nteeth_positional_mapping <- read.csv("results/nteeth_positional_mapping.csv")

nteeth_positional_mapping %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Locus,
    MarkerName,
    PIP,
    external_gene_name,
    ensembl_gene_id,
    everything()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>%
  datatable(
    caption = "Nteeth SuSiE Results with Nearest hg19 Genes",
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = list(
      dom = "Blfrtip",
      buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel", "pdf", "print"),
      lengthMenu = list(
        c(10, 25, 50, -1),
        c(10, 25, 50, "All")
      ),
      scrollX = TRUE
    )
  )

Nteeth eQTL colocalization

nteeth_eqtl_summary <- readLines("results/nteeth_eqtl_colocalization_summary.txt")

cat(nteeth_eqtl_summary)
No eQTL matches found in the specified tissue: Whole_Blood

Joint mvSuSiE Fine-Mapping: DMFS and Nteeth

This section presents the joint multi-trait fine-mapping results for DMFS and Nteeth using mvSuSiE-RSS. GWAS Z-scores for both traits were aligned to the same locus-specific EUR LD matrices. LD SNP identifiers were mapped from rsID to chr:position format using the corresponding .id_map files.

mvsusie_summary <- read.csv("results/dmfs_nteeth_mvsusie_summary.csv")

mvsusie_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Locus,
    rsid,
    MarkerName,
    CS,
    PIP,
    Purity,
    Z_DMFS,
    Z_Nteeth,
    Pvalue_DMFS,
    Pvalue_Nteeth,
    Effect_DMFS,
    Effect_Nteeth,
    everything()
  ) %>%
  datatable(
    extensions = "Buttons",
    caption = "Joint mvSuSiE Fine-Mapping Results for DMFS and Nteeth",
    options = list(
      dom = "Blfrtip",
      buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel", "pdf", "print"),
      lengthMenu = list(
        c(10, 25, 50, -1),
        c(10, 25, 50, "All")
      ),
      scrollX = TRUE
    )
  )

Top joint mvSuSiE signals

mvsusie_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Locus,
    rsid,
    MarkerName,
    CS,
    PIP,
    Purity,
    Z_DMFS,
    Z_Nteeth,
    Pvalue_DMFS,
    Pvalue_Nteeth
  ) %>%
  head(20)
